requirements: Source manifests and draft issue bodies must key off milestone codes and issue codes, not predicted GitHub numbers.
GitHub-assigned numeric identifiers must be accepted as returned and treated as publication artifacts, not planned inputs.
Live GitHub numbers may appear only in generated publication snapshots, reports, synchronization comments, or GitHub-native fields after publication.
If a published backlog packet references a live GitHub number, the reference must be refreshed from the live mapping report after publication rather than preserved from a prior draft.
blocked_by_issue_codes records direct issue blockers only.
direct_unblock_issue_codes records only the next directly unblocked issue codes.
Milestone descriptions may describe milestone-order sequencing, but issue bodies must not restate transitive blocker chains.
dependency_edges.json is the canonical publish-time source for GitHub built-in blocker edges.
GitHub built-in blocked-by links must mirror direct issue blockers only.
issue_body_rules: The Dependencies section lists direct issue blockers or none.
The Execution Order block lists only direct milestone blockers and direct issue blockers.
Do not embed future full-chain milestone sequences inside individual issue bodies.

scopes.internal-first: The milestone remains in the draft corpus and may exist on GitHub, but it is a publication-shaping or administrative tranche whose purpose is to improve the backlog itself before broader roadmap execution.
scopes.public-roadmap: The milestone is part of the normal externally legible roadmap and may be described as intended product or platform work.

requirements: M317 remains the only internal-first milestone in the current published tranche unless a later packet explicitly changes the policy.
Publication scope must be encoded in the manifest and reflected in issue labels.
Public-facing summaries must not describe internal-first milestones as end-user feature delivery milestones.
Each overlapping planning or publication family must name one active owner surface.
Superseded artifacts may remain in history or archives, but they must not remain active sources of truth.
When a surface is superseded, the replacement path and reason must be recorded in a generated inventory or policy-backed report.
Historical artifacts may be referenced as archival context only and must not be reopened as canonical inputs.
If a live GitHub milestone already exists for a stable milestone code, update and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate milestone with a guessed number.
If a live GitHub issue already exists for a stable issue code in the intended milestone, update and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate issue.
When new live milestones or issues must be created, accept the identifiers GitHub returns and then regenerate the live mapping snapshot so every downstream reference matches those assigned numbers.
Never attempt to reserve, predict, or preserve a desired GitHub numeric identifier.
New-work proposal publication and bulk backlog publication must run the live M318 governance preflight before calling GitHub APIs.
All count claims in planning summaries, milestone packets, and publication docs must come from generated snapshots or replayable measurement scripts.
Human summaries may quote counts only after the generating snapshot has been refreshed in the same rewrite pass.
If structural changes alter counts, the count snapshots and any derived human summaries must be regenerated before publication is considered complete.

implementation_notes: B002 should rewrite local backlog artifacts to consume this policy rather than restating it.
C001 should encode the manifest and artifact contract that makes these policy rules machine-checkable.
D001 should validate that generated issue markdown, publish JSON, and blocker graphs remain self-consistent after publication and should require the live governance preflight before any publish step.
non_goals: This policy does not define milestone-specific semantic or runtime governance outside backlog publication mechanics.
This policy does not require all historical tmp/archive artifacts to be deleted.
This policy does not guarantee that every GitHub field is represented in source manifests; it defines the minimum canonical backlog model.
